literal_meaning: quite secluded from sensuality, secluded from unskillful dhammas
preferred_translation: quite secluded from sensuality, secluded from unwholesome dhammas
alternative_translations: secluded from sensuality, secluded from unwholesome dhammas
discouraged_translations: withdrawn from sensual pleasures and evil states
definition: The standard opening formula for entry into the first mental theme.
notes: Use this record for the stock first-jhāna opening line. Keep kāma as sensuality rather than collapsing it into a narrower pleasures-only gloss, and keep akusala dhammas as unwholesome dhammas rather than inventing a different moral register each time the line appears.

example_phrases.translation: quite secluded from sensuality, secluded from unwholesome dhammas
example_phrases.notes: Standard first-jhāna entry line before the factor list and the delight-and-ease phrase.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use this as the standard English opening for the first mental theme unless a recorded local reason requires minor grammatical adjustment.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not narrow kāma to sensual pleasures only, and do not replace unwholesome dhammas with unrecorded moralizing or archaic variants.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Keeps the first-jhāna opening stable across DN 2 and the live translation surfaces that reuse the same entry formula.
